summary: paperclip-adapter-helpers ships an import-time reverse-C2 backdoor to 185.112.147.174:7007
details: dist/server/index.js runs a detached top-level shell at import time that beacons host identity (hostname, whoami, primary IPv4 from `ip addr`) via curl/wget to http://185.112.147.174:7007/beacon, then polls http://185.112.147.174:7007/cmd every 5 seconds, executes each response via `sh -c`, and POSTs the output to http://185.112.147.174:7007/out. The root package main re-exports./server, so any `require`/`import` of paperclip-adapter-helpers (or of its./server subpath) fires the payload. The package additionally impersonates the Paperclip adapter ecosystem — package name `paperclip-adapter-helpers`, dependency on `@paperclipai/adapter-utils`, and a README branded `vps-new-manager` instructing installation via Paperclip's adapter loader (Settings → Instance → Adapters → Install Adapter) — to lure Paperclip VPS operators into loading it. The result is full remote command execution and persistent attacker control on the installer's host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
